app.name: OpenCLI
app.one_liner: Turn websites, browser sessions, Electron apps, and local tools into deterministic interfaces for humans and AI agents.

screens.name: Main CLI Interface
screens.purpose: Provide command-line access to built-in and user-generated adapters for websites, Electron apps, and local binaries.
primary_actions: Execute site-specific CLI commands (e.g., hackernews top)

rust_commands.name: browser
rust_commands.purpose: Give AI agents direct browser control: click, type, extract, screenshot — any interaction, fully scriptable.

rust_commands.name: cascade
rust_commands.purpose: Find authentication strategies and workflow sequences during adapter synthesis.

rust_commands.name: explore
rust_commands.purpose: Discover APIs and behaviors from live browser sessions to inform adapter generation.

rust_commands.name: list
rust_commands.purpose: Enumerate all available CLI commands and adapters.

rust_commands.name: synthesize
rust_commands.purpose: Generate verified adapters from real browser behavior using structured contracts.

mvp_plan: week 1: Implement core `browser` command primitives (click, type, extract)
week 1: Integrate Browser Bridge extension with daemon
week 1: Support loading of built-in adapters for major sites (Bilibili, Twitter/X, Reddit)
acceptance_tests: AI agent can use `opencli browser` to navigate to a page, click an element, and extract text.
Anti-detection measures prevent detection by basic bot fingerprinting scripts.
Generated adapter from `explore` + `synthesize` produces deterministic CLI output on repeat runs.
Running `opencli hackernews top --limit 5` returns structured results without browser launch.
User can install the unpacked extension and run `opencli list` successfully.
